title: Micromeria glandulosa
taxonomy: Micromeria glandulosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. This plant is commonly classified as an herbaceous perennial, belonging to the genus Micromeria. The family Lamiaceae is a large and diverse group of plants, commonly known as the mint family. Within the Lamiaceae family, Micromeria glandulosa is part of the subfamily Nepetoideae. The exact classification of Micromeria glandulosa may vary slightly depending on the source.
uses: Micromeria glandulosa is a plant species known for its traditional medicinal uses, particularly in the region of the Balkans and Mediterranean. It has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in essential oils, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Micromeria glandulosa has been used to relieve symptoms of colds, coughs, and sore throats. Further research is needed to fully understand its potential health benefits and confirm its efficacy as a treatment for various ailments.

These compounds have been found to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The essential oil of Micromeria glandulosa is rich in sesquiterpenes, such as sesquiterpene hydrocarbons and oxygenated sesquiterpenes. intro: Micromeria glandulosa, also known as glandular micromeria or Spanish chamomile, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the Mediterranean region, belonging to the Lamiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been shown to potentially alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Micromeria glandulosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, particularly borneol, bornyl acetate, and borneol acetate, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Micromeria glandulosa include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, as well as to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
